_**User (2026-04-03 09:30:58Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is executing Phase 4 of the Inventorize project (multi-tenant SaaS inventory management app) using Claude Code. This is a continuation from previous sessions where Parts 1-3 were completed. The user's explicit request was "Start Phase 4 Part 4" which requires creating three packages: `packages/ui` (shadcn/ui component library), `packages/jobs` (BullMQ job queues), and `packages/storage` (S3-compatible file storage). The conversation resumed from a compacted context summary, and implementation proceeded without user interaction until the summary request.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Multi-tenant SaaS with subdirectory routing, PostgreSQL RLS active, all 6 security layers (L1-L6)
   - pnpm 10 monorepo with Turborepo v2, Node 22, ESM ("type": "module")
   - TypeScript strict mode with `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` (causes widespread type friction with optional props)
   - `.js` extensions required in ESM imports (not `.ts`) with `moduleResolution: "bundler"`
   - shadcn/ui component library pattern: Radix UI primitives + Tailwind CSS + class-variance-authority (cva)
   - BullMQ for job queues with ioredis connection, typed payloads requiring tenantId+userId on every job
   - Tenant-prefixed Valkey/Redis cache keys: `${tenantId}:${key}`
   - S3-compatible storage (MinIO for dev, S3 for prod) with tenant-scoped file paths: `${tenantId}/${entityType}/${uuid}${ext}`
   - MIME type validation: PDF/JPEG/PNG allowed, SVG/HTML explicitly blocked for XSS prevention
   - File download tenant verification via storage path prefix extraction
   - AsyncLocalStorage pattern for tenant context (established in Part 3)
   - Docker Hub: bonitobonita24/inventorize (publishing enabled)
   - Dev ports base=44874 (DB=44874, PGBOUNCER=44875, CACHE=44876, MINIO=44877, PGADMIN=44881, APP=44884, WORKER=44885, STUDIO=44894)
   - Git branching: scaffold/part-N for Phase 4, squash-merge to main

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **packages/ui/package.json** (CREATED)
     - Defines the UI component library with shadcn/ui dependencies
     - Uses wildcard exports for components: `"./components/*": "./src/components/*.tsx"`
     - Dependencies: @radix-ui/* (14 packages), class-variance-authority, clsx, lucide-react, tailwind-merge
     - Peer dependencies: react, react-dom, tailwindcss

   - **packages/ui/tsconfig.json** (CREATED, then MODIFIED)
     - Extends ../../tsconfig.base.json
     - Had to add `"lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]` to fix missing DOM types (HTMLTableCellElement etc.)

   - **packages/ui/src/lib/utils.ts** (CREATED)
     - cn() utility combining clsx + tailwind-merge
     ```ts
     import { type ClassValue, clsx } from 'clsx';
     import { twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge';
     export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]): string {
       return twMerge(clsx(inputs));
     }
     ```

   - **packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css** (CREATED)
     - Tailwind directives + CSS custom properties for light/dark theme
     - HSL-based color tokens: --background, --foreground, --primary, --secondary, --muted, --accent, --destructive, --border, --input, --ring, --radius

   - **packages/ui/src/components/** (16 components CREATED)
     - All use `../lib/utils.js` import (ESM .js extension)
     - All use React.forwardRef pattern for ref forwarding
     - button.tsx: cva variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) + sizes (default, sm, lg, icon), supports asChild via Slot
     - input.tsx: basic styled input
     - label.tsx: Radix Label + cva
     - card.tsx: Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter
     - table.tsx: Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableFooter, TableHead, TableRow, TableCell, TableCaption
     - badge.tsx: cva variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline)
     - dialog.tsx: Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription + overlay/portal/close
     - select.tsx: Full Select with scroll buttons, content, items, labels, separators
     - dropdown-menu.tsx: Full DropdownMenu with sub-menus, checkbox items, radio items, shortcuts — had `checked = false` default added for exactOptionalPropertyTypes
     - toast.tsx: ToastProvider, ToastViewport, Toast (with cva variants), ToastAction, ToastClose, ToastTitle, ToastDescription
     - separator.tsx: Radix Separator
     - avatar.tsx: Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback
     - checkbox.tsx: Radix Checkbox with Check icon
     - switch.tsx: Radix Switch
     - tabs.tsx: Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent
     - tooltip.tsx: TooltipProvider, Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent
     - skeleton.tsx: animate-pulse div
     - alert-dialog.tsx: Full AlertDialog with overlay, header, footer, action, cancel — imports buttonVariants from button.js

   - **packages/jobs/package.json** (CREATED)
     - Dependencies: bullmq ^5.20.0, ioredis ^5.4.0, @inventorize/shared workspace:*
     - Exports: `.`, `./queues`, `./workers`, `./cache`

   - **packages/jobs/tsconfig.json** (CREATED)
     - Extends ../../tsconfig.base.json, no extra libs needed

   - **packages/jobs/src/connection.ts** (CREATED)
     - Redis singleton with env var validation (REDIS_HOST, REDIS_PORT, REDIS_PASSWORD)
     - `maxRetriesPerRequest: null` and `enableReadyCheck: false` for BullMQ compatibility
     ```ts
     export function getRedisConnection(): Redis {
       if (sharedConnection !== null) return sharedConnection;
       const config = getRedisConfig();
       sharedConnection = new Redis({
         host: config.host, port: config.port, password: config.password,
         maxRetriesPerRequest: null, enableReadyCheck: false,
       });
       return sharedConnection;
     }
     ```

   - **packages/jobs/src/queues/types.ts** (CREATED)
     - LowStockCheckPayload: { tenantId, userId, triggeredAt }
     - EmailNotificationPayload: { tenantId, userId, type, recipientEmail, recipientName, subject, templateData }
     - QUEUE_NAMES const: LOW_STOCK_CHECK = 'low-stock-check', EMAIL_NOTIFICATIONS = 'email-notifications'

   - **packages/jobs/src/queues/low-stock-check.ts** (CREATED)
     - Queue singleton factory with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 5s delay), removeOnComplete: 1000, removeOnFail: 5000

   - **packages/jobs/src/queues/email-notifications.ts** (CREATED)
     - Queue singleton factory with exponential backoff (5 attempts, 3s delay), removeOnComplete: 2000, removeOnFail: 5000

   - **packages/jobs/src/workers/low-stock-check.worker.ts** (CREATED)
     - Worker factory accepting a processor function, concurrency: 1, rate limit: 10/min

   - **packages/jobs/src/workers/email-notifications.worker.ts** (CREATED)
     - Worker factory accepting a processor function, concurrency: 5, rate limit: 30/min

   - **packages/jobs/src/scheduler.ts** (CREATED)
     - Registers daily cron `0 0 * * *` for low-stock-check
     - Clears existing repeatable jobs before re-registering (idempotent)
     - Uses tenantId: 'ALL_TENANTS' marker for cron jobs (worker iterates tenants)

   - **packages/jobs/src/cache/index.ts** (CREATED)
     - Tenant-prefixed cache: `${tenantId}:${key}` pattern
     - Functions: cacheGet, cacheSet (with TTL), cacheDel, cacheGetJson<T>, cacheSetJson<T>

   - **packages/jobs/src/index.ts** (CREATED)
     - Barrel export of all connection, queue, worker, scheduler, and cache functions

   - **packages/storage/package.json** (CREATED)
     - Dependencies: @aws-sdk/client-s3 ^3.650.0, @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner ^3.650.0
     - Single export: "."

   - **packages/storage/tsconfig.json** (CREATED)
     - Extends ../../tsconfig.base.json

   - **packages/storage/src/client.ts** (CREATED)
     - S3Client singleton with env var validation (STORAGE_ENDPOINT, STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY, STORAGE_SECRET_KEY, STORAGE_BUCKET)
     - `forcePathStyle: true` for MinIO compatibility

   - **packages/storage/src/validation.ts** (CREATED, then MODIFIED)
     - ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES: application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png
     - BLOCKED_MIME_TYPES: image/svg+xml, text/html, application/xhtml+xml
     - MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES: 10MB
     - generateStoragePath: `${tenantId}/${entityType}/${uuid}${ext}` with randomized UUID filenames
     - extractTenantIdFromPath: extracts first path segment for tenant verification
     - Fix applied: `MIME_TO_EXT[mimeType] ?? (extname(originalFilename) || '.bin')` — parenthesized mixed ?? and || operators

   - **packages/storage/src/operations.ts** (CREATED)
     - uploadFile: validates MIME + size, generates storage path, PutObjectCommand
     - getDownloadUrl: verifies tenant ownership via path prefix, returns presigned URL (1h default)
     - deleteFile: verifies tenant ownership, DeleteObjectCommand
     - fileExists: verifies tenant ownership, HeadObjectCommand (returns false on mismatch instead of error)

   - **packages/storage/src/index.ts** (CREATED)
     - Barrel export of all client, validation, and operations

   - **docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md** (MODIFIED)
     - Added Part 4 entry with Agent: CLAUDE_CODE, listing all files added, errors encountered and resolved

   - **docs/IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md** (MODIFIED)
     - Phase updated to "Phase 4 Part 4 complete — ready for Part 5"
     - Part 4 section fully checked off with detailed line items for all 14 deliverables

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **exactOptionalPropertyTypes conflict with DropdownMenu checked prop** (packages/ui):
     - Error: `Type 'CheckedState | undefined' is not assignable to type 'CheckedState'` on the checked prop spread
     - Fix: Changed `({ className, children, checked, ...props }, ref)` to `({ className, children, checked = false, ...props }, ref)` — providing a default value prevents undefined from flowing through

   - **HTMLTableCellElement not found** (packages/ui):
     - Error: `Cannot find name 'HTMLTableCellElement'. Did you mean 'HTMLTableColElement'?` — also HTMLTableCaptionElement
     - Root cause: Base tsconfig only has `"lib": ["ES2022"]` without DOM types
     - Fix: Added `"lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"]` to packages/ui/tsconfig.json

   - **Mixed ?? and || operators** (packages/storage):
     - Error: `'??' and '||' operations cannot be mixed without parentheses`
     - Line: `const ext = MIME_TO_EXT[mimeType] ?? extname(originalFilename) || '.bin';`
     - Fix: Added parentheses: `MIME_TO_EXT[mimeType] ?? (extname(originalFilename) || '.bin')`

   - **msgpackr-extract build warning** from pnpm install — not addressed (low priority, from ioredis transitive dep)

5. Problem Solving:
   - The `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` setting continues to be a recurring source of type friction (seen in Parts 2, 3, and now 4). The pattern for fixing is to provide explicit default values for optional props instead of allowing undefined to flow through.
   - DOM type availability is package-specific — base tsconfig intentionally omits DOM types since not all packages need them (only UI does). Each package that needs DOM types must add them in its own tsconfig.
   - The monorepo typecheck runs all 6 packages in parallel via Turborepo and all pass cleanly after fixes.

6. All user messages:
   - "Start Phase 4 Part 4" (from compacted context — the explicit trigger for this work)
   - "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools..." (the summary request)

```text
1	# Inventorize
2	
3	## App Identity
4	Name:           Inventorize
5	Tagline:        Track your inventory, anytime, anywhere.
6	Industry:       SaaS / internal operations / inventory management
7	Primary users:  Platform super admin, tenant admins, warehouse staff, purchasing staff
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	Small and mid-size companies still track inventory in spreadsheets — leading to stock discrepancies, no audit trail, and zero visibility into stock movements. Existing inventory software is either too expensive, too complex, or requires on-premise installation. Inventorize provides a multi-tenant SaaS where each company gets its own isolated inventory workspace with barcode scanning, serial tracking, purchase orders, stock movement history, and full audit logging — all from a browser. The platform operator manages tenant onboarding, billing readiness, and cross-tenant health monitoring from a super admin dashboard.
11	
12	## Core User Flows
13	
14	1. **Super admin onboards a new tenant**: Super admin opens tenant management → creates new tenant (name, slug, contact email) → system provisions tenant workspace with subdirectory route (app.com/tenant-slug/) → super admin creates the tenant's first admin user account with tenant assignment → tenant admin receives welcome email → logs in and begins setup. Error: duplicate slug → block, show specific error. Error: user creation fails → roll back tenant record, show retry option.
15	
16	2. **Super admin manages the platform**: Super admin views all tenants list with status (active/suspended/trial) → can suspend or reactivate a tenant → can view cross-tenant metrics (total tenants, total products across platform, active users per tenant) → can impersonate a tenant admin for support (read-only mode) → all super admin actions create AuditLog entries. Error: suspending a tenant with active users → confirm prompt before proceeding.
17	
18	3. **Tenant admin creates a product**: Tenant admin opens product creation form → enters productCode, barcodeValue, name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled → system auto-calculates sellingPrice → save with currentQuantity = 0 → AuditLog created with tenantId. All data scoped to tenant automatically via RLS. Error: duplicate productCode within tenant → block save. Error: duplicate barcodeValue within tenant → block save.
19	
20	4. **Tenant admin edits a product**: Open existing product → edit allowed fields → recalculate sellingPrice if pricing changed → save → AuditLog with before/after values. Error: invalid values → block save. Note: currentQuantity never editable through this form.
21	
22	5. **Tenant admin or purchasing staff creates a purchase order**: Select supplier (tenant-scoped) → add products with ordered quantities → system snapshots supplierCost → optionally upload attachment → save as "draft" → later update status (ordered → partially_received → received, or cancel) → AuditLog on create and every status change. Error: no items → block. Error: attachment upload fails → preserve PO data, allow retry.
23	
24	6. **Any tenant staff records stock in**: Select product manually or scan product barcode via browser camera → enter quantity → optionally link PO → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/enter serial numbers (count must equal quantity) → optionally upload delivery receipt → system validates → increase Product.currentQuantity atomically → create SerialNumber records as "in_stock" if serial-tracked → create StockMovementLog → AuditLog → if linked to PO, update receivedQty and PO status. Error: duplicate serial for product within tenant → block. Error: any failure → roll back entire transaction.
25	
26	7. **Tenant admin or warehouse staff records stock out**: Select product manually or scan barcode → enter quantity, requestedByName, usedFor, optional notes → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/select exact serials (must be "in_stock", count must equal quantity) → validate sufficient stock → decrease Product.currentQuantity atomically → update serial status to "issued" → StockMovementLog → AuditLog → optionally generate printable slip. Error: insufficient stock → block, show available. Error: serial not in_stock → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
27	
28	8. **Tenant admin performs stock adjustment**: Enter reason and notes → select product → enter quantityDelta → if serial-tracked, identify serials → validate resulting stock not negative → apply atomically → StockMovementLog → AuditLog. approvedByUserId is optional audit trail only. Error: negative result → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
29	
30	9. **Any tenant user views product history**: Open product detail → view info, current quantity, serials if enabled → view StockMovementLog → filter by date, type, user, reference. Admin sees AuditLog entries. Pricing visibility enforced by role. All data tenant-scoped — users never see other tenants' data.
31	
32	10. **System runs low stock monitoring per tenant**: Daily scheduled job checks products per tenant where currentQuantity <= lowStockThreshold → per-tenant dashboard alert → low stock report → daily email to tenant admin and purchasing_staff → LowStockNotificationLog. Error: email fails → retry 3× with exponential backoff.
33	
34	11. **Any tenant user runs reports and exports**: Open dashboard or reports → apply filters → view data per role permissions → export as CSV → AuditLog for every export. All report data is tenant-scoped.
35	
36	## Modules + Features
37	
38	### Platform Administration (super_admin only)
39	- Tenant list with search, filter by status (active/suspended/trial)
40	- Tenant create form (name, slug, contact email)
41	- Tenant suspend / reactivate
42	- Cross-tenant metrics dashboard (total tenants, total products, active users per tenant)
43	- Tenant admin impersonation (read-only support mode)
44	- Platform-level audit log viewer
45	
46	### Dashboard (per tenant)
47	- KPI summary cards: total active products, total stock quantity, low stock count, stock in/out counts for period, inventory value (admin/purchasing only)
48	- Stock In vs Stock Out bar chart by period
49	- Low stock alert banner
50	
51	### Products
52	- Product list with search, filter, sort, pagination (tenant-scoped)
53	- Product create/edit form (tenant admin only)
54	- Product detail page with history tab
55	- Barcode scanning for quick product lookup
56	- Serial number list per product (if serial-tracked)
57	
58	### Suppliers
59	- Supplier list with search (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
60	- Supplier create/edit form (tenant-scoped)
61	
62	### Purchase Orders
63	- PO list with status filter (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
64	- PO create form with line items and attachment upload
65	- PO detail page with status management
66	- PO receiving (links to stock in)
67	
68	### Stock In
69	- Stock in form with barcode scanning
70	- PO linkage (optional)
71	- Serial number entry for serial-tracked products
72	- Delivery receipt upload
73	- Stock in history list
74	
75	### Stock Out
76	- Stock out form with barcode scanning
77	- Serial selection for serial-tracked products
78	- requestedByName and usedFor fields
79	- Printable stock out slip
80	- Stock out history list
81	
82	### Stock Adjustments
83	- Adjustment form (tenant admin only)
84	- Reason and notes required
85	- Positive or negative quantity delta
86	- Serial identification when applicable
87	- Adjustment history list
88	
89	### Reports
90	- Low Stock Report
91	- Stock Movement Report (filterable by date, product, type, user)
92	- Current Inventory Report
93	- Product History Report
94	- Audit Trail Report (tenant admin only, searchable)
95	- CSV export for all reports
96	
97	### Audit Logs
98	- Searchable audit log viewer (tenant admin only — tenant-scoped)
99	- Filter by action type, entity, user, date
100	
101	### Users
102	- User list (tenant admin only — shows only tenant's users)
103	- User create/edit/disable (no hard delete) — managed via Auth.js v5 with sessions in PostgreSQL
104	- Role assignment within tenant
105	
106	## Roles + Permissions
107	
108	| Role | Scope | Can do | Cannot do |
109	|------|-------|--------|-----------|
110	| super_admin | Platform-wide | Manage all tenants (create, suspend, reactivate). View cross-tenant metrics. Impersonate tenant admin (read-only). View platform audit logs. Create first admin user for new tenants. | Cannot modify tenant business data (products, stock, POs). Cannot create stock transactions. Cannot hard-delete tenants. |
111	| admin | Tenant-scoped | Manage users within own tenant (create, update, disable). Manage products (create, edit). View and update all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in/out. Create stock adjustments. View audit logs (own tenant only). View reports and export CSV. | Cannot see other tenants' data. Cannot manage platform. Cannot hard-delete users or products. Cannot directly edit currentQuantity. |
112	| warehouse_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products (selling price only). Create stock in. Create stock out. View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot view supplierCost or markupPercent. Cannot manage users, suppliers, POs, or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
113	| purchasing_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products with all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in. View stock out (read only). View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot create stock out. Cannot manage users or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
114	
115	## Data Entities
116	
117	### Platform-level (no tenantId)
118	- Tenant: id, name, slug (unique), contactEmail, status (enum: active/suspended/trial), createdAt, updatedAt
119	- User: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for super_admin), name, email, hashedPassword, role (enum: super_admin/admin/warehouse_staff/purchasing_staff), isActive, lastLoginAt, createdAt, updatedAt
120	
121	### Tenant-scoped (all have tenantId, enforced by RLS)
122	- Product: id, tenantId → Tenant, productCode (unique per tenant), barcodeValue (unique per tenant), name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice (computed), currentQuantity (system-managed), lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
123	- Supplier: id, tenantId → Tenant, name, contactPerson, phone, email, address, notes, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
124	- PurchaseOrder: id, tenantId → Tenant, poNumber (unique per tenant), supplierId → Supplier, orderDate, expectedDate, status (enum: draft/ordered/partially_received/received/cancelled), notes, attachmentUrl, createdByUserId → User, createdAt, updatedAt
125	- PurchaseOrderItem: id, purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder, productId → Product, orderedQty, receivedQty, supplierCostSnapshot
126	- StockIn: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder (optional), receivedDate, receivedByUserId → User, notes, attachmentUrl, createdAt
127	- StockInItem: id, stockInId → StockIn, productId → Product, quantity, supplierCostSnapshot
128	- StockOut: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), releasedDate, releasedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, notes, printableSlipNumber, createdAt
129	- StockOutItem: id, stockOutId → StockOut, productId → Product, quantity, sellingPriceSnapshot
130	- SerialNumber: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, serialValue, barcodeValue, status (enum: in_stock/issued/adjusted), stockInItemId → StockInItem (optional), stockOutItemId → StockOutItem (optional), createdAt, updatedAt. Unique constraint: [tenantId, productId, serialValue].
131	- StockAdjustment: id, tenantId → Tenant, adjustmentDate, reason, notes, createdByUserId → User, approvedByUserId → User (optional — audit trail only, not blocking), createdAt
132	- StockAdjustmentItem: id, stockAdjustmentId → StockAdjustment, productId → Product, quantityDelta, serialNumberId → SerialNumber (optional)
133	- StockMovementLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, movementType (enum: stock_in/stock_out/adjustment), referenceType, referenceId, quantityBefore, quantityDelta, quantityAfter, serialNumberId (optional), performedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, performedAt, notes — IMMUTABLE
134	- AuditLog: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for platform-level actions), actorUserId → User, actionType, entityType, entityId, fieldChangesJson, beforeStateJson, afterStateJson, createdAt — IMMUTABLE
135	- LowStockNotificationLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, notifiedToUserId → User, sentAt, status
136	
137	### Key Business Rules (CRITICAL — agents must enforce)
138	- Product.currentQuantity is system-managed only — never directly editable
139	- Product.sellingPrice is always computed: supplierCost + (supplierCost × markupPercent / 100)
140	- Stock can never go negative — any violating transaction must be blocked
141	- All stock quantity changes must be atomic — full transaction rollback on any failure
142	- Every quantity change must create a StockMovementLog with before/after values
143	- Every important action must create an AuditLog entry — immutable
144	- All tenant-scoped data must include tenantId — enforced by RLS at database level
145	- Users can never see, query, or modify another tenant's data
146	- Unique constraints (productCode, barcodeValue, poNumber, referenceNumber, serialValue) are scoped per tenant, not globally
147	- Serial tracking applies only when product has serialTrackingEnabled = true
148	- Barcode/QR scanning is input helper only — all scanned values must pass server-side validation
149	
150	## Integrations
151	- Email (SMTP): daily low stock notification emails per tenant, welcome emails for new tenant admins — SES in production, MailHog in dev
152	- No external auth service — Auth.js v5 sessions stored in PostgreSQL (white-label, zero external dependency)
153	
154	## Deployment Config
155	Environments: dev / staging / prod
156	Hosting:      Single VPS or managed services
157	Dev mode:     MODE A — WSL2 native (pre-locked)
158	Docker Hub:   enabled — hub_repo: bonitobonita24/inventorize
159	
160	## Mobile Needs
161	None — web only. The web app is mobile-first responsive (optimised for smartphones and tablets in warehouse environments) but there is no native mobile app.
162	
163	## Non-functional Requirements
164	Performance:    < 500ms API response for all CRUD operations. Stock transactions < 1s including all log writes. Tenant context resolution (slug → tenantId) < 10ms (cached).
165	Uptime:         99.5% SLA for production (SaaS — customer-facing)
166	Data retention: All traceability records kept minimum 7 years. Users disabled, never deleted. Tenants suspended, never deleted.
167	Compliance:     None specific. Standard data protection practices. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at database level via RLS.
168	
169	## Tenancy Model
170	multi — subdirectory routing
171	URL pattern: app.com/[tenant-slug]/dashboard, app.com/[tenant-slug]/products, etc.
172	Shared global data: none — all business data is tenant-scoped
173	Roles: global (same role set across all tenants: admin, warehouse_staff, purchasing_staff)
174	DB isolation: shared schema + tenant_id column on all tenant-scoped tables + PostgreSQL RLS
175	DB isolation exception: none (inventory data does not require separate schema)
176	
177	## User-Facing URLs
178	/login                              public — Auth.js v5 authentication
179	/platform/tenants                   super_admin — tenant management
180	/platform/metrics                   super_admin — cross-tenant metrics
181	/platform/audit-logs                super_admin — platform audit logs
182	/[tenant-slug]/dashboard            tenant — main dashboard
183	/[tenant-slug]/products             tenant — product list
184	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]        tenant — product detail
185	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]/history tenant — product movement history
186	/[tenant-slug]/suppliers            tenant — supplier list
187	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders      tenant — PO list
188	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders/[id] tenant — PO detail
189	/[tenant-slug]/stock-in             tenant — stock receiving
190	/[tenant-slug]/stock-out            tenant — stock releasing
191	/[tenant-slug]/adjustments          tenant — stock adjustments (admin only)
192	/[tenant-slug]/reports              tenant — reports and exports
193	/[tenant-slug]/audit-logs           tenant — audit log viewer (admin only)
194	/[tenant-slug]/users                tenant — user management (admin only)
195	
196	## Access Control
197	Public routes:    /login (Auth.js v5 sign-in page)
198	Protected routes: all other routes require authenticated session via Auth.js v5
199	Super-admin only: /platform/* routes
200	Tenant admin only: /[tenant-slug]/users, /[tenant-slug]/audit-logs, /[tenant-slug]/adjustments
201	Tenant admin + purchasing: /[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders, /[tenant-slug]/suppliers
202	All tenant roles: /[tenant-slug]/dashboard, /[tenant-slug]/products, /[tenant-slug]/stock-in, /[tenant-slug]/stock-out, /[tenant-slug]/reports
203	
204	### Tenant isolation rules
205	- Authenticated user's tenantId is extracted from Auth.js v5 session/JWT on every request (L1)
206	- Every tenant-scoped DB query is automatically filtered by tenantId via Prisma middleware (L2)
207	- PostgreSQL RLS provides database-level enforcement as defense-in-depth
208	- A user assigned to tenant A can never access routes or data for tenant B
209	- super_admin has no tenantId — accesses only /platform/* routes, never tenant business data directly
210	
211	## Data Sensitivity
212	PII stored:       yes — user name, email, hashed password (in PostgreSQL via Auth.js v5), requestedByName
213	Financial data:   yes — supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice, purchase order values, inventory valuation (restricted by role, scoped by tenant)
214	Health data:      no
215	Audit required:   tenant creation/suspension/reactivation, user creation/role changes/disabling, login events, product create/update/pricing/threshold changes, supplier create/update, PO create/update/status change, stock in, stock out, stock adjustment, serial number creation/status changes, file upload/removal, report export, notification send/failure, every quantity-affecting stock movement, super_admin impersonation events
216	GDPR/compliance:  no specific regulatory requirement — standard data protection. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at DB level. 7-year retention. No hard deletes.
217	
218	## Security Requirements
219	Rate limiting:    public: 60/min per IP | authenticated: 300/min per user | upload: 10/min per user
220	CORS origins:     dev: localhost:* | staging: https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app | prod: https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
221	Security layers:  L1 app-layer tenant scoping (tenantId from Auth.js session/JWT) — ACTIVE
222	                  L2 Prisma auto-inject middleware (tenantId on every query) — ACTIVE
223	                  L3 RBAC tRPC middleware (role-based access on every endpoint) — ACTIVE
224	                  L4 PgBouncer per-tenant connection pool limits — ACTIVE
225	                  L5 AuditLog (immutable record on every mutation) — ACTIVE
226	                  L6 Prisma query guardrails (automatic data scoping) — ACTIVE
227	
228	### Inventory-specific security
229	- No negative stock — enforced at database transaction level
230	- Audit logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
231	- Stock movement logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
232	- Tenant admin cannot view other tenants' audit logs
233	- Warehouse staff cannot view supplier cost or markup pricing data
234	- All barcode/QR scanned values must pass server-side validation
235	- super_admin actions are audit-logged separately at platform level
236	
237	### Browser camera scanning
238	- Camera permission requested before use
239	- Client-side JavaScript barcode/QR library (html5-qrcode or zxing-js)
240	- Supported: Android Chrome, iOS Safari, laptop webcam
241	- Manual text input fallback always available
242	- Live scanner preview with clear success/error feedback
243	- Large tap targets for warehouse mobile use
244	
245	## Domain / Base URL Expectations
246	Dev:     http://localhost:[port assigned by Phase 3 — do not specify a number here]
247	Stage:   https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app
248	Prod:    https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
249	
250	## Infrastructure Notes
251	Default: all services run in Docker Compose — mono-server for dev/staging/prod.
252	Docker Hub publishing: enabled — hub_repo: bonitobonita24/inventorize
253	pgAdmin: included on all environments — credentials auto-generated by Phase 3
254	CREDENTIALS.md: generated by Phase 3 — master credentials list for all envs, strictly gitignored
255	Security: HTTP headers + rate limiter + DOMPurify sanitizer scaffolded by Phase 4 — always-on defaults
256	Spec stress-test: Phase 2.7 runs automatically before Phase 3 — catches PRODUCT.md gaps early
257	AWS path when ready: RDS, S3, ElastiCache, SES — update .env.{env} only, zero code changes.
258	
259	### Compose services (dev)
260	- Next.js app (web server + tRPC API)
261	- PostgreSQL (primary database with RLS policies — also stores Auth.js v5 sessions)
262	- Valkey (cache + BullMQ job queue)
263	- MinIO (S3-compatible file storage for uploads)
264	- pgAdmin (database management UI)
265	- PgBouncer (connection pooling with per-tenant limits)
266	
267	### Production path
268	- Managed PostgreSQL with RLS (e.g. RDS, Supabase, or DigitalOcean Managed DB)
269	- S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, or DigitalOcean Spaces)
270	- Managed Redis/Valkey (e.g. ElastiCache, Upstash)
271	- PgBouncer (connection pooling)
272	- SES or equivalent for email notifications
273	
274	## Tech Stack Preferences
275	Frontend framework:        Next.js
276	API style:                 tRPC
277	ORM / DB layer:            Prisma
278	Auth provider:             Auth.js v5 (sessions in PostgreSQL — white-label, zero external service)
279	Auth strategy:             authjs
280	Primary database:          PostgreSQL
281	Cache / queue:             Valkey + BullMQ
282	File storage:              MinIO (dev) / S3 (prod)
283	UI component library:      shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
284	
285	## Design Identity
286	Brand feel:         professional/enterprise
287	Target aesthetic:   Clean, dense information layout optimised for fast warehouse operations. Minimal decorative elements. High contrast for readability in bright warehouse environments. Large tap targets and clear feedback for scanning workflows. Tenant branding kept minimal — platform-consistent UI.
288	Industry category:  SaaS / inventory management
289	Dark mode required: optional toggle
290	Key constraint:     Mobile-first responsive layout — warehouse staff primarily use smartphones and tablets. Scanner UI must have large tap targets and clear success/error feedback.
291	
292	## Out of Scope
293	- Multi-warehouse support per tenant (single location per tenant, no warehouse-to-warehouse transfers)
294	- Approval workflows (approvedByUserId on StockAdjustment is optional audit trail only — not blocking)
295	- Native mobile app (web app is mobile-first responsive, not a native iOS/Android app)
296	- Realtime push updates or WebSocket connections
297	- Advanced demand forecasting or predictive analytics
298	- Supplier analytics or supplier performance scoring
299	- Complex enterprise ERP features (accounting, general ledger, HR, payroll)
300	- Offline-first functionality (requires internet connection)
301	- Public API for external integrations
302	- Multi-language / i18n support
303	- Billing or invoicing (no subscription management, payment processing, or usage metering in this version)
304	- Customer self-service tenant registration (super_admin onboards tenants manually)
305	- Custom branding per tenant (single platform-wide look and feel)
306	- Tenant data export/migration tooling
307	- External IAM platform (no Logto, Auth0, or Clerk — Auth.js v5 handles all auth natively)
308
```

```text
84	    - name: email-notifications

86	      description: Sends welcome emails, low stock reports, and system notifications
```

```text
1	# Decisions Log — Spec-Driven Platform V26
2	# Format: ## YYYY-MM-DD — [Decision title]
3	# Types: 🟤 decision | ⚖️ trade-off
4	# Never re-ask anything listed here. These are locked decisions.
5	# ---
6	
7	## Dev Environment Mode
8	- Type:      🟤 decision
9	- Date:      2026-04-02
10	- Decision:  MODE A — WSL2 native (the only supported mode as of V25)
11	- Rationale: Devcontainer adds 4 virtualisation layers on WSL2 + Docker Desktop causing
12	             permission errors, shell server crashes, and socket failures. WSL2 native eliminates all of this.
13	             Docker Desktop provides the Docker socket to WSL2 natively. No DinD needed.
14	- Locked:    yes — do not re-ask or scaffold devcontainer files.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Git Branching Strategy
19	- Type:      🟤 decision
20	- Date:      2026-04-02
21	- Decision:  feat/{slug}, scaffold/part-{N}, squash-merge
22	- Rationale: Phase 4 uses part-by-part execution (Rule 24). Each Part gets its own branch.
23	             Feature branches use feat/{slug}. Never commit directly to main.
24	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 23
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Model Routing
29	- Type:      🟤 decision
30	- Date:      2026-04-02
31	- Decision:  Planning/execution/governance model assignments
32	- Rationale: Planning: claude-code (Phase 2) or minimax-m2.5 (Cline)
33	             Execution: minimax-m2.5 (default — free tier, Cline via OpenRouter)
34	             Governance: gemini-2.5-flash-lite (cheapest, non-critical writes)
35	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 24
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Port Strategy (Phase 3)
40	- Type:      🟤 decision
41	- Date:      2026-04-03
42	- Decision:  Non-standard random ports for all dev services. Base: 44874.
43	             DB=44874, PgBouncer=44875, Redis=44876, MinIO=44877, MinIO Console=44878,
44	             MailHog SMTP=44879, MailHog UI=44880, pgAdmin=44881, App=44884, Worker=44885, Studio=44894
45	- Rationale: Rule 22 — unique random ports per project prevent conflicts on developer machines.
46	             Standard ports (5432, 6379, etc.) reserved for staging/prod.
47	- Locked:    yes — regenerating ports requires updating all env files and restarting services.
48	
49	---
50	
51	## Docker Image Publishing
52	- Type:      🟤 decision
53	- Date:      2026-04-03 (updated)
54	- Decision:  docker.publish: true — Docker Hub pipeline enabled.
55	- Registry:  docker.io (Docker Hub)
56	- Repository: bonitobonita24/inventorize
57	- Image name: inventorize
58	- Tags:      latest (main branch) + sha-{short} (every push)
59	- Platforms: linux/amd64, linux/arm64
60	- Trigger:   push to main (GitHub Actions) + manual promotion (push.sh)
61	- Rationale: User corrected original decision — Docker Hub publishing is required.
62	- Secrets needed in GitHub repo:
63	    DOCKERHUB_USERNAME — Docker Hub username (bonitobonita24)
64	    DOCKERHUB_TOKEN    — Docker Hub access token (not password)
65	- GitHub Actions variable needed:
66	    DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME  — inventorize
67	- Locked:    yes
68	
69	---
70	
71	## Spec Stress-Test (Phase 2.7)
72	- Type:      🟤 decision
73	- Date:      2026-04-03
74	- Decision:  vibe_test.enabled: true — Phase 2.7 spec stress-test enabled.
75	- Rationale: Found 5 PRODUCT.md gaps before Phase 3: dashboard flow, impersonation enforcement,
76	             first-login mechanism, slug collision handling, printableSlipNumber format. All resolved.
77	- Locked:    yes — Phase 2.7 auto-runs before Phase 3 on any future spec update.
78	
79	---
80	
81	## First Admin Account
82	- Type:      🟤 decision
83	- Date:      2026-04-03
84	- Decision:  Username: webmaster, Role: super_admin. Password: AI-generated 22-char, stored in CREDENTIALS.md.
85	             Seeded by pnpm db:seed. Exists in ALL environments (dev, staging, prod).
86	- Rationale: App cannot be accessed without an initial admin account. Bootstrap credential gate (Step 18).
87	- Locked:    yes — never hardcode password in seed script. Always read from CREDENTIALS.md.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Tenant First-Login Mechanism
92	- Type:      🟤 decision
93	- Date:      2026-04-03
94	- Decision:  One-time setup token via /auth/setup?token=[token]. 24h expiry, single-use, stored as hash
95	             in VerificationToken table (Auth.js v5 compatible). Invalidated on use or expiry.
96	- Rationale: Phase 2.7 GAP 3 — welcome email login mechanism was undefined. Chosen: option A (password
97	             reset link) as it avoids sending a temporary plaintext password via email.
98	- Locked:    yes
99	
100	---
101	
102	## Tenant Slug Collision Handling
103	- Type:      🟤 decision
104	- Date:      2026-04-03
105	- Decision:  Auto-generate slug from tenant name (lowercased, special chars → hyphens). Editable field
106	             shown pre-filled. Real-time uniqueness check as super_admin types. Inline error on collision.
107	             Slug is permanent once tenant is created.
108	- Rationale: Phase 2.7 GAP 4 — slug collision handling was undefined. Chosen: editable field + inline
109	             error as it provides the most transparent UX without blocking the entire form.
110	- Locked:    yes
111	
112	---
113	
114	## StockOut Slip Number Format
115	- Type:      🟤 decision
116	- Date:      2026-04-03
117	- Decision:  Format: 'SO-' + zero-padded 5-digit sequential per tenant. e.g. SO-00001, SO-00002.
118	             Auto-generated at creation, immutable, unique per tenant. Counter never resets.
119	- Rationale: Phase 2.7 GAP 5 — printableSlipNumber format was undefined. Standard slip number
120	             format requested for warehouse operations.
121	- Locked:    yes
122
```

```text
1	// packages/db — Prisma client singleton with tenant context
2	// Re-exports PrismaClient and all generated types.
3	
4	import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';
5	import { tenantGuardExtension } from './middleware/tenant-guard.js';
6	
7	// Re-export tenant context utilities (defined in context.ts to avoid circular deps)
8	export {
9	  withTenantContext,
10	  getTenantContext,
11	  currentTenantId,
12	  currentUserId,
13	} from './context.js';
14	export type { TenantContext } from './context.js';
15	
16	// ─── Prisma client singleton ─────────────────────────────────
17	
18	const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as {
19	  prisma: ReturnType<typeof createPrismaClient> | undefined;
20	  platformPrisma: PrismaClient | undefined;
21	};
22	
23	function createPrismaClient() {
24	  const client = new PrismaClient({
25	    log:
26	      process.env['NODE_ENV'] === 'development'
27	        ? ['query', 'error', 'warn']
28	        : ['error'],
29	  });
30	  return client.$extends(tenantGuardExtension);
31	}
32	
33	/**
34	 * Tenant-scoped Prisma client (L6 guard active).
35	 * All queries automatically include tenantId from AsyncLocalStorage context.
36	 */
37	export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma ?? createPrismaClient();
38	
39	if (process.env['NODE_ENV'] !== 'production') {
40	  globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
41	}
42	
43	/**
44	 * Platform-level Prisma client WITHOUT tenant guard (for superadmin operations).
45	 * Use ONLY in platform-level routers. Never in tenant-scoped resolvers.
46	 * All operations logged with "PLATFORM:" prefix in AuditLog.
47	 */
48	export const platformPrisma =
49	  globalForPrisma.platformPrisma ??
50	  new PrismaClient({
51	    log:
52	      process.env['NODE_ENV'] === 'development'
53	        ? ['query', 'error', 'warn']
54	        : ['error'],
55	  });
56	
57	if (process.env['NODE_ENV'] !== 'production') {
58	  globalForPrisma.platformPrisma = platformPrisma;
59	}
60	
61	// ─── Re-exports ──────────────────────────────────────────────
62	export { PrismaClient, Prisma } from '@prisma/client';
63	export type {
64	  Tenant,
65	  User,
66	  VerificationToken,
67	  Product,
68	  Supplier,
69	  PurchaseOrder,
70	  PurchaseOrderItem,
71	  StockIn,
72	  StockInItem,
73	  StockOut,
74	  StockOutItem,
75	  SerialNumber,
76	  StockAdjustment,
77	  StockAdjustmentItem,
78	  StockMovementLog,
79	  AuditLog,
80	  LowStockNotificationLog,
81	  UserRole,
82	  TenantStatus,
83	  POStatus,
84	  SerialNumberStatus,
85	  MovementType,
86	  NotificationStatus,
87	  AdjustmentReason,
88	} from '@prisma/client';
89
```

```
# Stage 1 — install dependencies
FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN npm install -g pnpm
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml ./
COPY apps/web/package.json ./apps/web/
COPY packages/shared/package.json ./packages/shared/
COPY packages/db/package.json ./packages/db/
COPY packages/ui/package.json ./packages/ui/
COPY packages/jobs/package.json ./packages/jobs/
COPY packages/storage/package.json ./packages/storage/
COPY packages/api-client/package.json ./packages/api-client/
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Stage 2 — build
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=deps /app/apps/web/node_modules ./apps/web/node_modules
COPY --from=deps /app/packages/ ./packages/
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN pnpm --filter @inventorize/web build

# Stage 3 — minimal production runner
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder /app/apps/web/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder /app/apps/web/.next/static ./apps/web/.next/static
COPY --from=builder /app/apps/web/public ./apps/web/public
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
CMD ["node", "apps/web/server.js"]

```

```typescript
// Environment variable validation — fails fast at startup if any required var is missing
import { z } from 'zod';

const envSchema = z.object({
  // App
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'staging', 'production']).default('development'),
  APP_PORT: z.coerce.number().default(44884),

  // Database
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),

  // Auth
  AUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(32),
  NEXTAUTH_URL: z.string().url(),

  // Cache / Queue
  REDIS_HOST: z.string().min(1),
  REDIS_PORT: z.coerce.number(),
  REDIS_PASSWORD: z.string().min(1),

  // Storage
  STORAGE_ENDPOINT: z.string().url(),
  STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY: z.string().min(1),
  STORAGE_SECRET_KEY: z.string().min(1),
  STORAGE_BUCKET: z.string().min(1),
  STORAGE_REGION: z.string().default('us-east-1'),

  // Email
  SMTP_HOST: z.string().min(1),
  SMTP_PORT: z.coerce.number(),
  SMTP_FROM: z.string().min(1),
});

export type Env = z.infer<typeof envSchema>;

function validateEnv(): Env {
  const result = envSchema.safeParse(process.env);
  if (!result.success) {
    const formatted = result.error.flatten().fieldErrors;
    const missing = Object.entries(formatted)
      .map(([key, errors]) => `  ${key}: ${(errors ?? []).join(', ')}`)
      .join('\n');
    throw new Error(`Missing or invalid environment variables:\n${missing}`);
  }
  return result.data;
}

export const env = validateEnv();

```

```typescript
// Auth.js v5 configuration — credentials provider with PostgreSQL session storage
// Sessions stored in PostgreSQL via Prisma adapter (white-label, zero external auth service)

import NextAuth from 'next-auth';
import Credentials from 'next-auth/providers/credentials';
import { compare } from 'bcryptjs';
import { platformPrisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import type { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';

export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
  providers: [
    Credentials({
      name: 'credentials',
      credentials: {
        email: { label: 'Email', type: 'email' },
        password: { label: 'Password', type: 'password' },
      },
      async authorize(credentials) {
        if (typeof credentials?.email !== 'string' || typeof credentials?.password !== 'string') {
          return null;
        }

        const user = await platformPrisma.user.findFirst({
          where: { email: credentials.email, isActive: true },
          include: { tenant: true },
        });

        if (user === null) {
          return null;
        }

        const isValid = await compare(credentials.password, user.hashedPassword);
        if (!isValid) {
          return null;
        }

        // Update last login
        await platformPrisma.user.update({
          where: { id: user.id },
          data: { lastLoginAt: new Date() },
        });

        return {
          id: user.id,
          name: user.name,
          email: user.email,
          role: user.role as UserRole,
          tenantId: user.tenantId,
          tenantSlug: user.tenant?.slug ?? null,
        };
      },
    }),
  ],
  pages: {
    signIn: '/login',
    error: '/login',
  },
  session: {
    strategy: 'jwt',
    maxAge: 8 * 60 * 60, // 8 hours
  },
  callbacks: {
    jwt({ token, user }) {
      if (user !== undefined && user !== null) {
        token.userId = user.id;
        token.role = (user as Record<string, unknown>)['role'] as string;
        token.tenantId = (user as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantId'] as string | null;
        token.tenantSlug = (user as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantSlug'] as string | null;
      }
      return token;
    },
    session({ session, token }) {
      if (session.user !== undefined && session.user !== null) {
        session.user.id = token.userId as string;
        (session.user as Record<string, unknown>)['role'] = token.role;
        (session.user as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantId'] = token.tenantId;
        (session.user as Record<string, unknown>)['tenantSlug'] = token.tenantSlug;
      }
      return session;
    },
  },
});

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
// In-memory rate limiter — works for single-instance deployments (default).
// For multi-instance (multiple app containers): replace LRUCache with Redis store.

import { LRUCache } from 'lru-cache';
import { TRPCError } from '@trpc/server';

interface RateLimitOptions {
  uniqueTokenPerInterval?: number;
  interval?: number;
  limit?: number;
}

export function rateLimit(options?: RateLimitOptions) {
  const tokenCache = new LRUCache<string, number[]>({
    max: options?.uniqueTokenPerInterval ?? 500,
    ttl: options?.interval ?? 60_000,
  });

  return {
    check: (token: string, limit?: number) => {
      const maxRequests = limit ?? options?.limit ?? 60;
      const tokenCount = tokenCache.get(token) ?? [];
      const now = Date.now();
      const windowStart = now - (options?.interval ?? 60_000);
      const requestsInWindow = tokenCount.filter((t) => t > windowStart);

      if (requestsInWindow.length >= maxRequests) {
        throw new TRPCError({
          code: 'TOO_MANY_REQUESTS',
          message: 'Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.',
        });
      }

      tokenCache.set(token, [...requestsInWindow, now]);
    },
  };
}

export const rateLimiters = {
  public: rateLimit({ interval: 60_000, limit: 60 }),
  auth: rateLimit({ interval: 60_000, limit: 10 }),
  api: rateLimit({ interval: 60_000, limit: 300 }),
  upload: rateLimit({ interval: 60_000, limit: 10 }),
};

```

```typescript
// Strips dangerous HTML from user-submitted content before database storage.
// Prevents XSS — stored cross-site scripting attacks.

import DOMPurify from 'isomorphic-dompurify';

export function sanitize(dirty: string): string {
  return DOMPurify.sanitize(dirty, {
    ALLOWED_TAGS: ['b', 'i', 'em', 'strong', 'p', 'br', 'ul', 'ol', 'li', 'a'],
    ALLOWED_ATTR: ['href', 'target', 'rel'],
    FORCE_BODY: true,
  });
}

export function sanitizePlainText(dirty: string): string {
  return DOMPurify.sanitize(dirty, { ALLOWED_TAGS: [], ALLOWED_ATTR: [] });
}

```

```typescript
// tRPC context — created for every request, provides session + tenant info

import type { FetchCreateContextFnOptions } from '@trpc/server/adapters/fetch';
import { auth } from '@/server/auth/index.js';
import type { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';

export interface TRPCContext {
  session: {
    user: {
      id: string;
      name: string;
      email: string;
      role: UserRole;
      tenantId: string | null;
      tenantSlug: string | null;
    };
  } | null;
  userId: string | null;
  tenantId: string | null;
  roles: UserRole[];
  headers: Headers;
}

export async function createTRPCContext(
  opts: FetchCreateContextFnOptions,
): Promise<TRPCContext> {
  const session = await auth();

  const user = session?.user as {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    email: string;
    role: UserRole;
    tenantId: string | null;
    tenantSlug: string | null;
  } | undefined;

  return {
    session: user !== undefined
      ? { user }
      : null,
    userId: user?.id ?? null,
    tenantId: user?.tenantId ?? null,
    roles: user?.role !== undefined ? [user.role] : [],
    headers: opts.req.headers,
  };
}

```

```typescript
// Base tRPC initialization — procedures, middleware, error formatting

import { initTRPC, TRPCError } from '@trpc/server';
import superjson from 'superjson';
import type { TRPCContext } from './context.js';
import { rateLimiters } from '@/server/lib/rate-limit.js';

const t = initTRPC.context<TRPCContext>().create({
  transformer: superjson,
  errorFormatter({ shape, error }) {
    return {
      ...shape,
      data: {
        ...shape.data,
        // Never expose internal error details in production
        stack: process.env['NODE_ENV'] === 'development' ? error.cause?.stack : undefined,
      },
    };
  },
});

export const createCallerFactory = t.createCallerFactory;
export const createTRPCRouter = t.router;
export const mergeRouters = t.mergeRouters;

// Rate-limited public procedure
export const publicProcedure = t.procedure.use(async ({ ctx, next }) => {
  const ip = ctx.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ?? 'unknown';
  rateLimiters.public.check(ip);
  return next({ ctx });
});

// Auth middleware — rejects unauthenticated requests
const enforceAuth = t.middleware(({ ctx, next }) => {
  if (ctx.session === null) {
    throw new TRPCError({ code: 'UNAUTHORIZED', message: 'Not authenticated.' });
  }
  return next({
    ctx: {
      ...ctx,
      session: ctx.session,
      userId: ctx.session.user.id,
      tenantId: ctx.session.user.tenantId,
      roles: [ctx.session.user.role],
    },
  });
});

// Rate-limited authenticated procedure
export const protectedProcedure = t.procedure.use(async ({ ctx, next }) => {
  const token = ctx.userId ?? ctx.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ?? 'unknown';
  rateLimiters.api.check(token);
  return next({ ctx });
}).use(enforceAuth);

// Tenant-scoped procedure — requires auth + valid tenantId
const enforceTenant = t.middleware(({ ctx, next }) => {
  if (ctx.tenantId === null) {
    throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Access denied.' });
  }
  return next({
    ctx: {
      ...ctx,
      tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
    },
  });
});

export const tenantProcedure = protectedProcedure.use(enforceTenant);

```

```typescript
// L3 RBAC middleware — role-based access control on every endpoint

import { TRPCError } from '@trpc/server';
import { initTRPC } from '@trpc/server';
import type { TRPCContext } from '../context.js';
import { type UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';

const t = initTRPC.context<TRPCContext>().create();

export const requireRole = (...allowedRoles: UserRole[]) =>
  t.middleware(({ ctx, next }) => {
    if (ctx.session === null) {
      throw new TRPCError({ code: 'UNAUTHORIZED', message: 'Not authenticated.' });
    }

    const userRole = ctx.session.user.role;
    const hasRole = allowedRoles.includes(userRole);
    if (!hasRole) {
      throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Access denied.' });
    }

    return next({ ctx });
  });

```

```typescript
// L1 tenant scoping middleware — ensures tenantId present in context

import { TRPCError } from '@trpc/server';
import { initTRPC } from '@trpc/server';
import type { TRPCContext } from '../context.js';

const t = initTRPC.context<TRPCContext>().create();

export const requireTenant = t.middleware(({ ctx, next }) => {
  if (ctx.tenantId === null) {
    throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Access denied.' });
  }

  return next({
    ctx: {
      ...ctx,
      tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
    },
  });
});

// Impersonation guard — blocks mutations when isImpersonating is true
export const blockIfImpersonating = t.middleware(({ ctx, next }) => {
  const session = ctx.session;
  if (session !== null && (session.user as Record<string, unknown>)['isImpersonating'] === true) {
    throw new TRPCError({
      code: 'FORBIDDEN',
      message: 'Mutations are blocked during impersonation.',
    });
  }
  return next({ ctx });
});

```

```typescript
// Product router — tenant-scoped CRUD + search + history

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc.js';
import { requireRole } from '../middleware/rbac.js';
import { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';

export const productRouter = createTRPCRouter({
  list: tenantProcedure
    .input(
      z.object({
        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
        search: z.string().optional(),
        category: z.string().optional(),
        isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const where = {
            tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
            ...(input.search !== undefined
              ? {
                  OR: [
                    { name: { contains: input.search, mode: 'insensitive' as const } },
                    { productCode: { contains: input.search, mode: 'insensitive' as const } },
                    { barcodeValue: { contains: input.search, mode: 'insensitive' as const } },
                  ],
                }
              : {}),
            ...(input.category !== undefined ? { category: input.category } : {}),
            ...(input.isActive !== undefined ? { isActive: input.isActive } : {}),
          };

          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
            prisma.product.findMany({
              where,
              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
              take: input.limit,
              orderBy: { name: 'asc' },
              select: {
                id: true,
                productCode: true,
                barcodeValue: true,
                name: true,
                category: true,
                unit: true,
                currentQuantity: true,
                lowStockThreshold: true,
                serialTrackingEnabled: true,
                isActive: true,
                // Pricing visible based on role — warehouse_staff excluded
                ...(ctx.roles.includes(UserRole.WAREHOUSE_STAFF)
                  ? { sellingPrice: true }
                  : {
                      supplierCost: true,
                      markupPercent: true,
                      sellingPrice: true,
                    }),
              },
            }),
            prisma.product.count({ where }),
          ]);

          return {
            items,
            total,
            page: input.page,
            limit: input.limit,
            totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit),
          };
        },
      );
    }),

  byId: tenantProcedure
    .input(z.object({ id: z.string().cuid() }).strict())
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const product = await prisma.product.findFirst({
            where: { id: input.id, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
          });
          if (product === null) {
            return null;
          }
          return product;
        },
      );
    }),

  create: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN))
    .input(
      z.object({
        productCode: z.string().min(1).max(50),
        barcodeValue: z.string().min(1).max(100),
        name: z.string().min(1).max(200),
        category: z.string().min(1).max(100),
        unit: z.string().min(1).max(50),
        supplierCost: z.number().min(0),
        markupPercent: z.number().min(0),
        lowStockThreshold: z.number().int().min(0),
        serialTrackingEnabled: z.boolean(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const sellingPrice = input.supplierCost + (input.supplierCost * input.markupPercent / 100);

          return prisma.product.create({
            data: {
              ...input,
              tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
              sellingPrice,
              currentQuantity: 0,
              isActive: true,
            },
          });
        },
      );
    }),

  update: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN))
    .input(
      z.object({
        id: z.string().cuid(),
        productCode: z.string().min(1).max(50).optional(),
        barcodeValue: z.string().min(1).max(100).optional(),
        name: z.string().min(1).max(200).optional(),
        category: z.string().min(1).max(100).optional(),
        unit: z.string().min(1).max(50).optional(),
        supplierCost: z.number().min(0).optional(),
        markupPercent: z.number().min(0).optional(),
        lowStockThreshold: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),
        serialTrackingEnabled: z.boolean().optional(),
        isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const { id, ...data } = input;

          // Recalculate sellingPrice if pricing fields changed
          const updateData: Record<string, unknown> = { ...data };
          if (data.supplierCost !== undefined || data.markupPercent !== undefined) {
            const existing = await prisma.product.findFirst({
              where: { id, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
            });
            if (existing === null) {
              throw new Error('Resource not found.');
            }
            const cost = data.supplierCost ?? existing.supplierCost.toNumber();
            const markup = data.markupPercent ?? existing.markupPercent.toNumber();
            updateData['sellingPrice'] = cost + (cost * markup / 100);
          }

          return prisma.product.update({
            where: { id },
            data: updateData,
          });
        },
      );
    }),
});

```

```typescript
// Supplier router — tenant-scoped CRUD

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc.js';
import { requireRole } from '../middleware/rbac.js';
import { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';

export const supplierRouter = createTRPCRouter({
  list: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
        search: z.string().optional(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const where = {
            tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
            ...(input.search !== undefined
              ? {
                  OR: [
                    { name: { contains: input.search, mode: 'insensitive' as const } },
                    { contactPerson: { contains: input.search, mode: 'insensitive' as const } },
                  ],
                }
              : {}),
          };
          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
            prisma.supplier.findMany({
              where,
              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
              take: input.limit,
              orderBy: { name: 'asc' },
            }),
            prisma.supplier.count({ where }),
          ]);
          return { items, total, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit) };
        },
      );
    }),

  create: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        name: z.string().min(1).max(200),
        contactPerson: z.string().max(200).nullable().default(null),
        phone: z.string().max(50).nullable().default(null),
        email: z.string().email().nullable().default(null),
        address: z.string().max(500).nullable().default(null),
        notes: z.string().max(2000).nullable().default(null),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          return prisma.supplier.create({
            data: { ...input, tenantId: ctx.tenantId, isActive: true },
          });
        },
      );
    }),

  update: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        id: z.string().cuid(),
        name: z.string().min(1).max(200).optional(),
        contactPerson: z.string().max(200).nullable().optional(),
        phone: z.string().max(50).nullable().optional(),
        email: z.string().email().nullable().optional(),
        address: z.string().max(500).nullable().optional(),
        notes: z.string().max(2000).nullable().optional(),
        isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const { id, ...data } = input;
          return prisma.supplier.update({ where: { id }, data });
        },
      );
    }),
});
```

```typescript
// Purchase order router — tenant-scoped CRUD + status management

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc.js';
import { requireRole } from '../middleware/rbac.js';
import { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';

export const purchaseOrderRouter = createTRPCRouter({
  list: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
        status: z.string().optional(),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const where = {
            tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
            ...(input.status !== undefined ? { status: input.status } : {}),
          };
          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
            prisma.purchaseOrder.findMany({
              where,
              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
              take: input.limit,
              orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
              include: { supplier: { select: { name: true } }, items: true },
            }),
            prisma.purchaseOrder.count({ where }),
          ]);
          return { items, total, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit) };
        },
      );
    }),

  byId: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(z.object({ id: z.string().cuid() }).strict())
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          return prisma.purchaseOrder.findFirst({
            where: { id: input.id, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
            include: {
              supplier: true,
              items: { include: { product: { select: { name: true, productCode: true } } } },
            },
          });
        },
      );
    }),

  create: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        supplierId: z.string().cuid(),
        orderDate: z.string().datetime(),
        expectedDate: z.string().datetime().nullable().default(null),
        notes: z.string().max(2000).nullable().default(null),
        items: z.array(
          z.object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            orderedQty: z.number().int().min(1),
            supplierCostSnapshot: z.number().min(0),
          }).strict(),
        ).min(1),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const { items, ...poData } = input;
          return prisma.purchaseOrder.create({
            data: {
              ...poData,
              tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
              poNumber: `PO-${Date.now()}`,
              status: 'draft',
              createdByUserId: ctx.userId,
              items: { create: items.map((item) => ({ ...item, receivedQty: 0 })) },
            },
            include: { items: true },
          });
        },
      );
    }),

  updateStatus: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        id: z.string().cuid(),
        status: z.enum(['ordered', 'partially_received', 'received', 'cancelled']),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          return prisma.purchaseOrder.update({
            where: { id: input.id },
            data: { status: input.status },
          });
        },
      );
    }),
});
```

```typescript
// Stock in router — tenant-scoped receiving with atomic quantity updates

import { z } from 'zod';
import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc.js';
import { requireRole } from '../middleware/rbac.js';
import { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';

export const stockInRouter = createTRPCRouter({
  list: tenantProcedure
    .input(
      z.object({
        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const where = { tenantId: ctx.tenantId };
          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
            prisma.stockIn.findMany({
              where,
              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
              take: input.limit,
              orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
              include: { items: { include: { product: { select: { name: true } } } } },
            }),
            prisma.stockIn.count({ where }),
          ]);
          return { items, total, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit) };
        },
      );
    }),

  create: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.WAREHOUSE_STAFF, UserRole.PURCHASING_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        purchaseOrderId: z.string().cuid().nullable().default(null),
        receivedDate: z.string().datetime(),
        notes: z.string().max(2000).nullable().default(null),
        items: z.array(
          z.object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            quantity: z.number().int().min(1),
            supplierCostSnapshot: z.number().min(0),
          }).strict(),
        ).min(1),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          // Atomic transaction — stock in + quantity update + movement log
          return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
            const stockIn = await tx.stockIn.create({
              data: {
                tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
                referenceNumber: `SI-${Date.now()}`,
                purchaseOrderId: input.purchaseOrderId,
                receivedDate: new Date(input.receivedDate),
                receivedByUserId: ctx.userId,
                notes: input.notes,
                items: { create: input.items },
              },
              include: { items: true },
            });

            // Update product quantities and create movement logs
            for (const item of stockIn.items) {
              const product = await tx.product.findFirstOrThrow({
                where: { id: item.productId, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
              });

              await tx.product.update({
                where: { id: item.productId },
                data: { currentQuantity: { increment: item.quantity } },
              });

              await tx.stockMovementLog.create({
                data: {
                  tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
                  productId: item.productId,
                  movementType: 'stock_in',
                  referenceType: 'StockIn',
                  referenceId: stockIn.id,
                  quantityBefore: product.currentQuantity,
                  quantityDelta: item.quantity,
                  quantityAfter: product.currentQuantity + item.quantity,
                  performedByUserId: ctx.userId,
                  performedAt: new Date(),
                },
              });
            }

            return stockIn;
          });
        },
      );
    }),
});
```

```typescript
// Stock out router — tenant-scoped releasing with atomic quantity updates

import { z } from 'zod';
import { TRPCError } from '@trpc/server';
import { createTRPCRouter, tenantProcedure } from '../trpc.js';
import { requireRole } from '../middleware/rbac.js';
import { UserRole } from '@inventorize/shared/enums';
import { prisma } from '@inventorize/db';
import { withTenantContext } from '@inventorize/db';

export const stockOutRouter = createTRPCRouter({
  list: tenantProcedure
    .input(
      z.object({
        page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
        limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          const where = { tenantId: ctx.tenantId };
          const [items, total] = await Promise.all([
            prisma.stockOut.findMany({
              where,
              skip: (input.page - 1) * input.limit,
              take: input.limit,
              orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
              include: { items: { include: { product: { select: { name: true } } } } },
            }),
            prisma.stockOut.count({ where }),
          ]);
          return { items, total, page: input.page, limit: input.limit, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / input.limit) };
        },
      );
    }),

  create: tenantProcedure
    .use(requireRole(UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.WAREHOUSE_STAFF))
    .input(
      z.object({
        releasedDate: z.string().datetime(),
        requestedByName: z.string().min(1).max(200),
        usedFor: z.string().min(1).max(500),
        notes: z.string().max(2000).nullable().default(null),
        items: z.array(
          z.object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            quantity: z.number().int().min(1),
            sellingPriceSnapshot: z.number().min(0),
          }).strict(),
        ).min(1),
      }).strict(),
    )
    .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {
      return withTenantContext(
        { tenantId: ctx.tenantId, userId: ctx.userId },
        async () => {
          return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
            // Generate slip number (SO-00001 format, per-tenant sequential)
            const lastOut = await tx.stockOut.findFirst({
              where: { tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
              orderBy: { printableSlipNumber: 'desc' },
              select: { printableSlipNumber: true },
            });
            const lastNum = lastOut !== null
              ? parseInt(lastOut.printableSlipNumber.replace('SO-', ''), 10)
              : 0;
            const slipNumber = `SO-${String(lastNum + 1).padStart(5, '0')}`;

            // Validate sufficient stock for all items
            for (const item of input.items) {
              const product = await tx.product.findFirstOrThrow({
                where: { id: item.productId, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
              });
              if (product.currentQuantity < item.quantity) {
                throw new TRPCError({
                  code: 'BAD_REQUEST',
                  message: `Insufficient stock for ${product.name}. Available: ${product.currentQuantity}`,
                });
              }
            }

            const stockOut = await tx.stockOut.create({
              data: {
                tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
                referenceNumber: `SO-REF-${Date.now()}`,
                releasedDate: new Date(input.releasedDate),
                releasedByUserId: ctx.userId,
                requestedByName: input.requestedByName,
                usedFor: input.usedFor,
                notes: input.notes,
                printableSlipNumber: slipNumber,
                items: { create: input.items },
              },
              include: { items: true },
            });

            // Decrement quantities and create movement logs
            for (const item of stockOut.items) {
              const product = await tx.product.findFirstOrThrow({
                where: { id: item.productId, tenantId: ctx.tenantId },
              });

              await tx.product.update({
                where: { id: item.productId },
                data: { currentQuantity: { decrement: item.quantity } },
              });

              await tx.stockMovementLog.create({
                data: {
                  tenantId: ctx.tenantId,
                  productId: item.productId,
                  movementType: 'stock_out',
                  referenceType: 'StockOut',
                  referenceId: stockOut.id,
                  quantityBefore: product.currentQuantity,
                  quantityDelta: -item.quantity,
                  quantityAfter: product.currentQuantity - item.quantity,
                  performedByUserId: ctx.userId,
                  requestedByName: input.requestedByName,
                  usedFor: input.usedFor,
                  performedAt: new Date(),
                },
              });
            }

            return stockOut;
          });
        },
      );
    }),
});
```
